channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: TikTok is full of bogus, potentially dangerous medical advice
item.description: TikTok is the new doctor’s office, quickly becoming a go-to platform for medical advice. Unfortunately, much of that advice is sketchy at best. A new report by the healthcare software firm Tebra found 45% of medical advice on TikTok to be false or misleading.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: The greatest keyboard never sold
item.description: Even as the latest phones and wearables tout speech recognition with unprecedented accuracy and spatial computing products flirt with replacing tablets and laptops, physical keyboards remain beloved for their efficiency. Earlier this year, for example, sci-fi novelist Robert J.
item.dc:creator: Ross Rubin, Fast Company

item.title: Tech expert warns of device that disables Wi-Fi cameras
item.description: It just takes a flick of a switch, and it can make your Wi-Fi camera stop working and miss the crucial video you need.
item.dc:creator: Elena Cousino, NewsNation

item.title: AI will eavesdrop on world's wildest places to track and help protect endangered wildlife
item.description: A biologist hid 350 audio monitors across Costa Rica’s tropical rainforests to spy on endangered spider monkeys in order to help protect them.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Say hello to SetMe: A modern solution for offering remote tech support
item.description: SetMe is a remote support solution for businesses of all sizes that allows IT teams to easily secure a connection to remote computers, allowing for ad-hoc or unattended support that was designed with years of experience in remote support software from the team behind FixMe.IT.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: McDonalds delivery customers put at risk by possible data breach
item.description: Delivery system for McDonalds in India had a worrying bug, but luckily, threat actors didn't find it.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Trump pushes back on 'President Musk' claims
item.description: President-elect Trump on Sunday rejected the recent dubbing of tech billionaire Elon Musk as “President Musk” by Democrats. “No, he’s not gonna be president, that I can tell you,” Trump said at conservative group Turning Point's “America Fest” in Phoenix, Ariz.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Top 5 mistakes that could expose your financial data to cybercriminals
item.description: There are common habits and oversights that could leave your financial information vulnerable to cybercriminals, according to Kurt the CyberGuy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NASA's Parker Solar Probe aims to fly closer to the sun like never before
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A NASA spacecraft aims to fly closer to the sun than any object sent before. The Parker Solar Probe was launched in 2018 to get a close-up look at the sun. Since then, it has flown straight through the sun's corona: the outer atmosphere visible during a total solar eclipse.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Amazon and Starbucks workers are on strike. Trump might have something to do with it
item.description: Amazon delivery drivers and Starbucks baristas are on strike in a handful of U.S. cities as they seek to exert pressure on the two major companies to recognize them as unionized employees or to meet demands for an inaugural labor contract.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Albania to close TikTok for a year blaming it for promoting violence among children
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — Albania's prime minister said Saturday the government will shut down the video service TikTok for one year, blaming it for inciting violence and bullying, especially among children.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Andrew Ng is betting big on agentic AI
item.description: Andrew Ng came up with a theory almost 15 years ago: What if Google, the internet giant, thought of machines like the brain and trained neural networks on mass amounts of data using computational power in an attempt to lead to artificial general intelligence? Google bit on Ng’s line of thinking, an
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Do you need a VPN at home? Here are 10 reasons you do
item.description: A virtual private network can help ensure your information remains security and your privacy remains intact. Kurt the CyberGuy explains.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amazon workers are striking at multiple facilities. Here's what you should know
item.description: Amazon workers affiliated with the Teamsters union continued a strike Friday at seven of the company's delivery hubs just days before Christmas. At midnight on Saturday, workers at a prominent unionized warehouse in New York will also join, the Teamsters said.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: More CISA staff would keep working under DHS’ revised shutdown plan
item.description: Under a new plan, about one-third of CISA's staff will keep working through a shutdown. But experts are still concerned about the impact on U.S. cyber defenses. The post More CISA staff would keep working under DHS’ revised shutdown plan first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Why OMB’s human-centered policy design effort is paying off
item.description: Federal CIO Clare Martorana’s tenure is coming to an end in January and she reflects back on her office’s efforts to improve federal technology. The post Why OMB’s human-centered policy design effort is paying off first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Amazon workers strike at multiple facilities as Teamsters seek labor contract
item.description: Workers at seven Amazon facilities went on strike Thursday, an effort by the Teamsters union to pressure the e-commerce company for a labor agreement during a key shopping period.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Create searchable Bluesky bookmarks with R
item.description: The Bluesky social network is seeping into the mainstream, thanks in part to people unhappy with changes at X (formerly Twitter). “It’s not some untouched paradise, but it’s got a spark of something refreshing,” Jan Beger, global head of AI Advocacy at GE HealthCare, posted on LinkedIn last month.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: FAQ: Getting started with Bluesky
item.description: What is Bluesky? Bluesky is a “microblogging” social media network similar to X (formerly Twitter), where people can publish posts with a maximum of 300 characters, as well as follow others, create lists, like posts, and so on.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Data on animal movements help Hungarian researchers create a swarm of autonomous drones
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Moving in a dense cloud, like throngs of people walking across a crowded public square, 100 drones maneuver through the night sky in Hungary's capital, the result of over a decade of research and experimentation that scientists believe could change the future of unmanned fli
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: GitHub launches free tier of Copilot AI coding assistant
item.description: GitHub has launched GitHub Copilot Free, providing access to a free tier of the AI-based pair programmer via Microsoft’s Visual Studio Code editor. Announced December 18, Copilot Free gives developers access to 2,000 code completions and 50 chat messages monthly.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Musk and Trump are viewed roughly the same by Americans, an AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk, clad in tuxedo and black tie, took the stage at President-elect Donald Trump's Mar-a-Lago resort shortly after the election with all the swagger of the winning candidate himself. “The public has given us a mandate that could not be more clear, the clearest mandate.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, SARAH PARVINI and AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: OpenAI responds to Elon Musk's lawsuit
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CompTIA launches expert-level cybersecurity certification
item.description: CompTIA this week made available its CompTIA SecurityX certification program, which is an updated version of the CompTIA Advanced Security Practitioner (CASP+) cert and part of CompTIA’s Xpert Series of certifications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: From CAPTCHA to catastrophe: How fake verification pages are spreading malware
item.description: CAPTCHAs, which are used by websites to confirm whether users are people or bots, are harmless, but hackers are using them to infect PCs with malware.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Youtube has a new tool to detect AI-faked celebrities
item.description: Fake AI clips of celebrities are becoming increasingly common, something that affects, among other things, artists and actors who have their voices and looks cloned without their permission.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
